mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 40% of cases when FEDFUNDS is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when UNRATE is below 4.0% in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative when both GDPC1 and INDPRO are rising in more than 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when FEDFUNDS increases by >0.5% in 3 months in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 200k jobs per month signals robust labor market demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when PAYEMS exceeds 200k in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when INDPRO rises in more than 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal strong consumer and business demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST rise in more than 25% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation regimes, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when FEDFUNDS rises in high inflation periods in more than 50% of cases.
